description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from gray stone blocks, featuring three prominent archways: a large central arch flanked by two smaller side arches. The structure is rectangular and robust, with a thick, solid top and vertical sides, all composed of tightly fitted stonework. The surface texture is rough and uneven, characteristic of traditional masonry. There are no visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels, giving the arch a utilitarian and austere appearance. The archways are open, allowing passage through the structure, and the overall design is reminiscent of Roman architectural styles. The sides of the arch are plain, with the stone block pattern continuing uninterrupted.
description: A gray stone triumphal arch with three open archways and a rough, blocky masonry texture. The design is simple and Roman-inspired, with no visible decorations.
description_view_0: Front view showing three open archways framed by rough stone blocks.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thick, solid stone wall with no visible openings.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with three open archways and stone block texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the solid stone wall with no openings.
